Feature: Automated Credit Scoring Engine for Consumer Loans

The lending division needs an AI-powered credit scoring engine that evaluates
loan applications from individual consumers. The system will:

- Ingest applicant financial data: income verification, existing debt levels,
  payment history from credit bureaus, bank transaction patterns
- Analyze alternative data sources including utility payment records and
  rental payment history for thin-file applicants
- Process personal identification data for KYC verification
- Generate a proprietary credit score (300-850 scale) that determines
  loan eligibility, interest rate tier, and maximum loan amount
- Automatically approve applications scoring above 680, flag applications
  between 580-680 for manual review, and automatically reject below 580

The credit score directly determines whether a natural person receives a
consumer loan and at what terms. The system processes sensitive financial
and personal data of approximately 50,000 applicants per month.

The AI system operates with human-on-the-loop oversight: a credit analyst
reviews the flagged mid-range applications but the auto-approve and
auto-reject decisions require no human intervention. The system is deployed
as a cloud-hosted service accessible to our branch offices across Austria
and Germany.

This replaces our legacy rule-based scoring system and is expected to
improve default prediction accuracy by 23%.
